definition_plain: Interpretability is the study of understanding why a model does what it does — opening the black box to find which internal features activate for which concepts, and why a given input produced a given output. It is frontier safety work: if you can understand what a model is 'thinking,' you can catch misaligned behaviour before it causes harm.
source_label: Elhage et al., 2022 — Toy Models of Superposition
